entities.name: GROUNDNUT
entities.description: The target crop, Arachis hypogaea L., used in the breeding study to combine high oleic acid and disease resistance.

entities.name: HIGH OLEIC ACID
entities.description: An industry-preferred trait that enhances economic returns, targeted for introgression into groundnut cultivars.

entities.name: RUST RESISTANCE
entities.description: The ability of groundnut to resist rust disease, a key breeding objective.

entities.name: LATE LEAF SPOT RESISTANCE
entities.description: The ability of groundnut to resist late leaf spot (LLS) disease, a key breeding objective.

entities.name: RUST
entities.description: A disease that causes significant economic loss in groundnut crops.

entities.name: LATE LEAF SPOT
entities.description: A disease that causes significant economic loss in groundnut crops.

entities.name: MARKER-ASSISTED SELECTION
entities.description: A breeding technique used to introgress high oleic content and disease resistance into the Kadiri 6 cultivar.

entities.name: KADIRI 6
entities.description: A popular groundnut cultivar used as the genetic background for introgression of target traits.

entities.name: SIMPLE SEQUENCE REPEATS
entities.description: Linked allele-specific markers used to select for target traits during the breeding process.

entities.name: SINGLE NUCLEOTIDE POLYMORPHIC MARKERS
entities.description: Genetic markers used to identify and select for target traits in groundnut lines.

entities.name: NEAR-INFRARED REFLECTANCE SPECTROSCOPY
entities.description: A phenotyping technique used to identify groundnut lines with oleic acid content greater than 80%.
